response:
Let’s check if 16469 is prime.

First, note that \( \sqrt{16469} \approx 128.33 \), so we only need to test primes up to 127.

Check divisibility by small primes:  
- Not even (last digit 9).  
- Sum of digits: \( 1+6+4+6+9 = 26 \), not divisible by 3.  
- Last digit not 0 or 5, so not divisible by 5.  
- Check 7: \( 16469 \div 7 \approx 2352.714... \) not integer.  
- Check 11: alternating sum \( 1 - 6 + 4 - 6 + 9 = 2 \), not divisible by 11.  
- Check 13: \( 13 \times 1267 = 16471 \) (too high), \( 13 \times 1266 = 16458 \) (difference 11), so no.  
- Check 17: \( 17 \times 969 = 16473 \) (too high), \( 17 \times 968 = 16456 \) (difference 13), so no.  
- Check 19: \( 19 \times 867 = 16473 \) (too high), \( 19 \times 866 = 16454 \) (difference 15), so no.  
- Check 23: \( 23 \times 716 = 16468 \) (difference 1), so no.  
- Check 29: \( 29 \times 568 = 16472 \) (too high), \( 29 \times 567 = 16443 \) (difference 26), so no.  
- Check 31: \( 31 \times 531 = 16461 \) (difference 8), so no.  
- Check 37: \( 37 \times 445 = 16465 \) (difference 4), so no.  
- Check 41: \( 41 \times 401 = 16441 \) (difference 28), so no.  
- Check 43: \( 43 \times 383 = 16469 \) exactly.

Since \( 43 \times 383 = 16469 \), it is divisible by 43 and 383, so it is **not prime**.

**Answer:** no
